jokes.text: Don't make fun of Paula. She's the only woman short enough to be my wife.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending Paula Abdul from mockery
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because she's short enough for him to marry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton line - his earnest romanticism combined with self-aware height insecurity. Economical structure with unexpected reveal.

jokes.text: Work sucks. But you already know that, don't you?
jokes.setup: Hilary complaining about work to the butler
jokes.punchline: Casually acknowledges that Geoffrey's work life must suck too
jokes.explanation: Hilary's oblivious privilege showing through - she's so self-absorbed she accidentally acknowledges Geoffrey's situation.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm young, I'm happening, I'm groovy. Why do I have the sudden urge to tie dye my drawers and go down to the hootenanny?
jokes.setup: Philip claiming he's young and hip
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses extremely dated slang that proves the opposite
jokes.explanation: Philip's complete lack of self-awareness perfectly executed. The outdated slang immediately undermines his claim to youth.

jokes.text: First he loses one client, then another, and another... and the next thing you know, we're all working at Domino's.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining the domino theory of business failure
jokes.punchline: The domino theory literally leads to working at Domino's pizza
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - the economic concept plus the literal pizza chain, with perfect escalation structure.

jokes.text: You'll fit right in. You're used to cutting the cheese.
jokes.setup: Context of working at Domino's pizza
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of cutting cheese (pizza) and flatulence
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile wordplay that relies on cheap double entendre. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: All that denim will take his clients' eyes off his bald spot.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting Philip buy stone washed jeans
jokes.punchline: Will suggests it's strategic distraction from baldness
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about middle-aged fashion choices as distraction tactics.

jokes.text: That's more like a hair cul-de-sac.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Philip's receding hairline
jokes.punchline: Comparing balding pattern to suburban street layout
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that's both original and perfectly economical. The suburbia reference fits the Bel-Air setting perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's also hereditary.
jokes.setup: Philip saying baldness is dignified
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton will inherit it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor targeting Carlton's future, delivered with perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: Hot diggity dog, where have you been all my life?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey meeting attractive neighbor Karen
jokes.punchline: His overly enthusiastic, old-fashioned exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - his formal demeanor cracking with old-fashioned enthusiasm.

jokes.text: That's not all you can do, Mr. French.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey introducing himself as the butler
jokes.punchline: Karen's flirtatious response with TV butler reference
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference to Family Affair's Mr. French, shows Karen's flirtatiousness.

jokes.text: I was wondering if you could lend me a cup of Lysol?
jokes.setup: Neighbor asking to borrow something
jokes.punchline: Asking for cleaning fluid instead of typical sugar/flour
jokes.explanation: Subverts the typical 'cup of sugar' neighbor trope with cleaning supplies, fitting the domestic worker theme.

jokes.text: Like I give a damn.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking about the regular housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Karen's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Sets up Karen's casual attitude, becomes running gag when repeated.

jokes.text: Like I give a damn.
jokes.setup: Another question about domestic arrangements
jokes.punchline: Exact repetition of previous dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Callback strengthens the running gag, shows Karen's consistent dismissiveness.

jokes.text: And your buns aren't bad either.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey commenting on the croissants looking good
jokes.punchline: Karen's double entendre about buns meaning buttocks
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre setup with food/anatomy wordplay, predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: You read my mind.
jokes.setup: Karen's buns comment followed by Geoffrey offering buns
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's innocent response showing he missed the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Geoffrey's innocence contrasted with Karen's boldness.

jokes.text: Bad, teatime.
jokes.setup: Will interrupting Geoffrey and Karen's intimate tea moment
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward acknowledgment of the interruption timing
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart awareness of romantic timing, economically delivered.

jokes.text: Dust rags, furniture wax, a massage.
jokes.setup: Karen offering to help Geoffrey with cleaning supplies
jokes.punchline: Escalates from innocent supplies to suggestive massage
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation from domestic to intimate, showing Karen's forward nature.

jokes.text: My God. I hope I didn't say that out loud.
jokes.setup: Karen's suggestive massage comment
jokes.punchline: Her horrified realization she said it aloud
jokes.explanation: Classic 'did I say that out loud' trope, predictable but fits the awkward romantic setup.

jokes.text: Looks like G just got busy, English style.
jokes.setup: Will observing Geoffrey's romantic encounter
jokes.punchline: Will's hip interpretation with ethnic twist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will voice - translating Geoffrey's proper romance into street vernacular.

jokes.text: G, you're wiping the table with a croissant, man.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey distracted after Karen leaves
jokes.punchline: Visual of using food as cleaning implement
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag showing Geoffrey's love-struck confusion through absurd action.

jokes.text: Philip Banks, this is your hair.
jokes.setup: Carlton presenting a toupee solution
jokes.punchline: Formal introduction as if the hair is a person
jokes.explanation: Carlton's earnest formality applied to absurd situation creates perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Say, is that Philip Banks, or Blair Underwood?
jokes.setup: Carlton admiring Philip in the toupee
jokes.punchline: Comparing bald lawyer to handsome actor
jokes.explanation: Flattering but obviously false comparison, shows Carlton's enthusiasm for the makeover.

jokes.text: I look like Little Richard, Attorney at Law.
jokes.setup: Philip trying on different toupees
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to flamboyant musician in legal context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of Little Richard's wild persona with Philip's staid legal profession. Perfectly economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Great. Where's the fish? Spoiling off the coast of Nova Scotia?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey presents elaborate condiments for fish dinner
jokes.punchline: Philip notes the fish itself is missing, suggests it's still swimming
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Geoffrey's lovesick distraction affecting his work performance.

jokes.text: Has Geoffrey been sniffing the 409?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's erratic behavior forgetting the main course
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he's inhaling cleaning chemicals
jokes.explanation: Philip's practical interpretation of Geoffrey's distracted behavior, fits his character's directness.

jokes.text: No, his nose is open.
jokes.setup: Philip's confusion about Geoffrey's behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's street slang explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will voice using street vernacular that the family doesn't understand.

jokes.text: Who am I kidding? My nose is as open as a 7-Eleven.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey alone, admitting his romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: Using Will's slang with British twist about always-open convenience stores
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Geoffrey adopting Will's slang while maintaining his own voice. The 7-Eleven comparison is both accurate and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: She's so cultured, she's so refined. She can read.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey listing Karen's sophisticated qualities
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic revelation that basic literacy impresses him
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that deflates at the end, revealing Geoffrey's low standards due to infatuation.

jokes.text: If you ain't Barry White, you ain't got to beg.
jokes.setup: Will advising Geoffrey not to say 'please'
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference to smooth R&B singer as dating advice
jokes.explanation: Great Will advice mixing street wisdom with pop culture reference. The Barry White comparison is perfect for dating context.

jokes.text: Geoffrey breaking every rule Will just gave him during the phone call
jokes.setup: Will's specific dating advice about what not to say
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately does everything Will told him not to do
jokes.explanation: Extended visual gag showing Geoffrey's inability to follow simple dating advice due to his earnest nature.

jokes.text: The last housekeeper I dated turned up in a T-shirt that said 'Loose Booty.'
jokes.setup: Geoffrey appreciating Karen's refined nature and similar station
jokes.punchline: Revealing his previous dating experience was less classy
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Geoffrey's relationship history, the T-shirt detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I want to take you in my arms like a load of fresh laundry, hot from the dryer.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey attempting romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Using domestic imagery for romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey romance - his domestic work life bleeding into his romantic expressions. Original and character-specific.

jokes.text: That's me, you know, please, don't squeeze the Caruthers.
jokes.setup: Karen revealed as toilet tissue heiress
jokes.punchline: Parodying Charmin's advertising slogan with her family name
jokes.explanation: Clever advertising parody that explains the wealth reveal while being family-friendly about toilet paper.

jokes.text: She dies.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey watching Love Story while heartbroken
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact spoiler
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan spoiler delivery by Will, economical and fits his direct personality.

jokes.text: Damn, what is my point?
jokes.setup: Will explaining why Geoffrey shouldn't date someone richer
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-argument that wealthy women buying things sounds great
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where Will realizes his own argument makes no sense, shows his honest personality.

jokes.text: Lunch is served. But it sucks.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's formal announcement of meal service
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining his own professional presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of Geoffrey's professional duties with his depression, extremely economical.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? He's our butler.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to take Geoffrey to nice restaurant
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious class distinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her complete lack of awareness about class and empathy.

jokes.text: Is that G or Blind Lemon Pledge?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey singing the blues while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison to blues musician mixed with cleaning product
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mashup of Blind Lemon Jefferson (blues musician) and Pledge (furniture polish), perfect for Geoffrey's situation.

jokes.text: have you ever had somebody come into your life and suddenly... it's as if a door opens and everything goes from drab black and white to glorious color. And then just as suddenly, she's gone. The door slams in your face, and you're left standing alone in the dark.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explaining his heartbreak to Will
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic, dramatic description of dating someone briefly
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's overwrought romantic poetry creates humor through excessive drama for a brief relationship.

jokes.text: That's the saddest thing I ever heard.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's dramatic romantic soliloquy
jokes.punchline: Will's genuine emotional response
jokes.explanation: Will's sincere reaction to Geoffrey's overwrought speech shows their relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: G is dying to talk to you. Ain't you, G?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey specifically asked Will to say he doesn't want to talk
jokes.punchline: Will immediately does the opposite
jokes.explanation: Will's helpful meddling backfires, typical of his good intentions gone wrong.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, 25 mil?
jokes.setup: Learning Karen is worth $25 million
jokes.punchline: Will's delayed reaction to the massive wealth
jokes.explanation: Will's materialistic side showing through as he processes the enormous wealth figure.

jokes.text: Hey, hold it, can I get that limo?
jokes.setup: Will arranging Geoffrey's date logistics
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to benefit from the wealthy girlfriend's resources
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will opportunism, always looking for personal advantage in others' situations.

jokes.text: Whatever you do, don't scratch. I didn't bring any extra glue.
jokes.setup: Philip wearing the toupee to the party
jokes.punchline: Carlton's practical concern about the hairpiece maintenance
jokes.explanation: Carlton's practical nature showing through, treating the toupee like a craft project needing maintenance.

jokes.text: Nothing's pulled into my station for quite a while.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentioning Karen should think of her station (social class)
jokes.punchline: Karen interprets 'station' sexually as train station metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning playing on Geoffrey's formal language and Karen's boldness.

jokes.text: Geoffrey Butler of the South Hampton Butlers.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey introducing himself by profession
jokes.punchline: Rich guest misunderstanding, thinking it's his last name
jokes.explanation: Misunderstanding creates temporary class elevation for Geoffrey, shows rich people's assumptions.

jokes.text: He is so considerate. Put that tray down.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey automatically serving at the party
jokes.punchline: Karen's contradiction - praising him then ordering him to stop serving
jokes.explanation: Karen's mixed signals about Geoffrey's class position, showing the awkwardness of their situation.

jokes.text: I stayed with my Uncle Neville and his common law wife, Ruby.
jokes.setup: Asked if he stayed at fancy hotel in England
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's working-class family arrangement
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's honesty about his humble background contrasts with the rich guests' assumptions.

jokes.text: Getting this water spot off your fork.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey acting like a butler at the party
jokes.punchline: His compulsive cleaning behavior embarrassing Karen
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey can't help reverting to his professional habits, creating social awkwardness.

jokes.text: They're all folded the wrong way.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey continuing his compulsive butler behavior
jokes.punchline: Criticizing the party's napkin folding standards
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's professional standards overwhelming his social situation, escalating the embarrassment.

jokes.text: My hip hurts?
jokes.setup: Will asking what hip thing Philip said
jokes.punchline: Vivian's literal interpretation of 'hip'
jokes.explanation: Vivian's wordplay misunderstanding, showing generational gap with slang usage.

jokes.text: Yo, Vivian, I can't ride my Harley right now, I think it'll mess up my hair.
jokes.setup: Recalling Philip's attempt to be hip
jokes.punchline: Philip's concern for his toupee preventing motorcycle riding
jokes.explanation: Philip's midlife crisis behavior limited by his vanity about the toupee.

jokes.text: You know I can water ski in this puppy.
jokes.setup: Philip's confidence in his toupee's durability
jokes.punchline: Suggesting extreme sports compatibility with hairpiece
jokes.explanation: Philip's overconfidence in his toupee's adhesive properties creates absurd scenario.

jokes.text: Carlton, you are grounded until you start losing your hair.
jokes.setup: Philip removing toupee and accepting his baldness
jokes.punchline: Punishment tied to Carlton's future hair loss
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the hereditary baldness joke, creative punishment that acknowledges genetics.

jokes.text: But there ain't no leaves, G. You see leaves, I see leaves, man.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey pretending to sweep leaves as excuse to watch Karen's house
jokes.punchline: Will's patient accommodation of Geoffrey's obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Will's kindness in playing along with Geoffrey's transparent excuse shows their friendship.

jokes.text: What difference does it make if she's richer than a bus load of Japanese tourists?
jokes.setup: Will arguing money shouldn't matter in relationships
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical reference to wealthy tourists
jokes.explanation: Relies on dated stereotype for humor, doesn't add much to the scene.

jokes.text: You wear a tux to clean the toilet.
jokes.setup: Will arguing Geoffrey has plenty of class
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Geoffrey's formal dress for menial tasks
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about Geoffrey's character - his dignity in all situations. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: But the honey, like, moonlighted as a topless dancer.
jokes.setup: Will creating hypothetical scenarios about class and dating
jokes.punchline: Adding scandalous occupation to diplomatic daughter scenario
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt to confuse Geoffrey's class system with increasingly complex scenarios.

jokes.text: You're pretty much full of it, G.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's convoluted explanation of British class system
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment of Geoffrey's excuses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - cutting through Geoffrey's British formality with street wisdom.

jokes.text: I think it's time for me to go squeeze the Caruthers.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey deciding to pursue Karen despite class differences
jokes.punchline: Using the toilet paper slogan as romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that transforms the advertising slogan into Geoffrey's romantic resolve.

jokes.text: I should never have let something as foolish as $25,000,000 come between us.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's romantic apology about letting money interfere
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of the enormous sum as 'foolish'
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's British understatement treating massive wealth as a minor consideration.

jokes.text: Great. I own a beach. You bring the snow cones.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggesting simple romantic date walking on beach
jokes.punchline: Karen casually revealing she owns the beach
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Geoffrey's modest romantic fantasy with Karen's casual wealth display.

jokes.text: Take a break, Jeeves.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey and Karen kissing romantically
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to stereotypical butler name
jokes.explanation: Standard butler reference, fits Will's tendency to comment on romantic moments.
